name: components
description: Extended component library supplementing uikit with higher-level components: adaptive tabs, changelog dialog, confirm dialog, cookie consent, notifications, onboarding menu, reactions, share popover, stories, and more.
when_to_use: Extended component library supplementing uikit with higher-level components: adaptive tabs, changelog dialog, confirm dialog, cookie consent, notifications, onboarding menu, reactions, share popover, stories, and more
avoid: Not a replacement for uikit — extends it with app-level patterns. Not for AI interfaces (use aikit) or navigation shell (use navigation).

name: AdaptiveTabs
description: Renders a responsive tab list that automatically hides overflowing tabs behind a "N more" dropdown select when the container is too narrow. When only one tab fits, all tabs collapse into a single select control.
when_to_use: When you have a fixed-width or resizable container and need tabs that gracefully handle overflow
When the number of tabs is dynamic or unknown at design time
When you need configurable per-breakpoint maximum tab widths
avoid: Avoid when the number of tabs is small and guaranteed to fit — use uikit Tabs directly for simplicity
Avoid for vertical navigation layouts

props.name: items
props.description: Array of tab items. Each item must have an id; title and disabled are optional.

props.name: onSelectTab
props.description: Callback fired when the user selects a tab.

props.name: activeTab
props.description: ID of the currently active tab.

props.name: allowNotSelected
props.description: When true, no tab needs to be active.

props.name: breakpointsConfig
props.description: Map of container-width breakpoints to maximum tab widths (percent). Defaults to built-in config.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root container.

props.name: moreControlProps
props.description: Props forwarded to the hidden-tabs Select popup.

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the tabs. Defaults to 'm'.

props.name: wrapTo
props.type: (item: TabItem<T> | undefined, node: React.ReactNode, index: number | undefined) => React.ReactNode
props.description: Wraps each tab node, useful for adding router links or custom wrappers.

examples:
<AdaptiveTabs
  items={[
    {id: 'a', title: 'First'},
    {id: 'b', title: 'Second'},
    {id: 'c', title: 'Third', disabled: true},
  ]}
  activeTab={activeTab}
  onSelectTab={(id) => setActiveTab(id)}
/>

(
extra {node}
)} />

<AdaptiveTabs
  items={items}
  activeTab={activeTab}
  onSelectTab={setActiveTab}
  breakpointsConfig={{400: 33, 900: 25, 1400: 20}}
  size="l"
/>

name: ChangelogDialog
description: Displays a modal dialog listing application changelog entries (versions). Each entry can include a date, title, description, image, and an optional link to a related story or full changelog.
when_to_use: When you need to show users a list of recent product changes or release notes in a modal
When you want to highlight new features with images and story links
avoid: Avoid for inline changelog displays — this renders a full modal dialog
Avoid when entries do not require images or story links; a simple list may be sufficient

props.name: items
props.description: List of changelog entries to display.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Called when the dialog requests to close.

props.name: open
props.description: Controls dialog visibility.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class.

props.name: disableBodyScrollLock
props.description: When true (default), body scrolling is not locked while the dialog is open.

props.name: disableHeightTransition
props.description: Disables height transition animation. Defaults to true.

props.name: disableOutsideClick
props.description: Prevents closing on outside click.

props.name: error
props.description: Displays an error state instead of items.

props.name: fullListLink
props.description: URL shown as a link to the full changelog.

props.name: loading
props.description: Shows a loading spinner instead of items.

props.name: onLinkClick
props.description: Called when a link inside an item is clicked.

props.name: onRetryClick
props.description: Called when the error state retry button is clicked.

props.name: onStoryClick
props.description: Called when a 'View story' link is clicked.

props.name: title
props.description: Dialog title. Defaults to 'Changelog'.

examples:
<ChangelogDialog
  open={open}
  onClose={() => setOpen(false)}
  items={[
    {
      date: '03 Jul 2022',
      isNew: true,
      title: 'New navigation',
      image: {src: 'https://example.com/img.png', ratio: 240/516},
      description: 'New navigation description',
      storyId: 'storyId1',
    },
    {
      date: '15 Jun 2022',
      title: 'Minor fixes',
      description: 'Bug fixes and improvements',
    },
  ]}
/>

name: ConfirmDialog
description: A utility modal dialog for confirming or cancelling a user action. Renders a title, message, and apply/cancel buttons via the uikit Dialog component.
when_to_use: When you need to ask the user to confirm a potentially destructive or irreversible action
When you need a simple two-button (apply/cancel) confirmation modal
avoid: Avoid for complex forms or multi-step confirmations — use a full Dialog instead
Avoid when no user confirmation is needed before the action

props.name: onClickButtonApply
props.description: Called when the apply/confirm button is clicked.

props.name: onClickButtonCancel
props.description: Called when the cancel button is clicked.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Called when the dialog is closed via escape key or outside click.

props.name: textButtonApply
props.description: Label for the apply/confirm button.

props.name: textButtonCancel
props.description: Label for the cancel button.

props.name: message
props.description: The confirmation message body.

props.name: propsButtonApply
props.description: Additional props forwarded to the apply button.

props.name: propsButtonCancel
props.description: Additional props forwarded to the cancel button.

props.name: title
props.description: Dialog title.

examples:
<ConfirmDialog
  open={open}
  title="Do you want to confirm?"
  message="This action cannot be undone."
  textButtonApply="Yes"
  textButtonCancel="No"
  onClickButtonApply={() => { handleConfirm(); setOpen(false); }}
  onClickButtonCancel={() => setOpen(false)}
  onClose={() => setOpen(false)}
/>

name: CookieConsent
description: Renders a GDPR/cookie consent banner or popup after a short delay, based on the mode configured in the provided ConsentManager. Supports notification bar, full management popup, and simple accept/decline modes.
when_to_use: When your service collects user analytics or tracking cookies and needs GDPR-compliant consent
When you need to let users manage their cookie preferences (necessary vs analytics)
avoid: Avoid if your service does not use cookies requiring user consent
Do not use without a ConsentManager instance — the component requires it to function

props.name: consentManager
props.description: An IConsentManager instance controlling cookie mode, state, and persistence.

props.name: manageCookies
props.description: When true, opens the cookie management popup immediately regardless of consent state.

props.name: onConsentPopupClose
props.description: Called when the consent popup or banner is closed.

props.name: buttonAcceptText
props.description: Custom text for the accept button (popup/simple modes).

props.name: buttonDeclineText
props.description: Custom text for the decline button (popup/simple modes).

props.name: buttonOkText
props.description: Custom text for the OK button (notification mode).

props.name: policyLink
props.description: URL of the privacy policy page.

props.name: policyLinkText
props.description: Display text for the privacy policy link.

props.name: text
props.description: Consent banner body text.

examples:
const consentManager = new ConsentManager('base');

<CookieConsent consentManager={consentManager} />

name: DelayedTextInput
description: A controlled text input that debounces the onUpdate callback by a configurable delay. Useful for search fields where you want to avoid triggering network requests on every keystroke.
when_to_use: When you need to debounce user text input before triggering expensive operations like API searches
When you want the UI to feel instant while deferring state updates
avoid: Avoid when immediate (undebounced) onChange handling is required
Avoid when an uncontrolled input suffices — this requires both value and onUpdate to be provided

props.name: onUpdate
props.description: Called with the debounced value after the delay.

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled input value.

props.name: delay
props.description: Debounce delay in milliseconds. Defaults to 200.

name: FormRow
description: Lays out a form field with an accessible label, optional required indicator, and optional help popover in a configurable row or column direction. Also exposes FormRow.FieldDescription for hint text below the field.
when_to_use: When building forms that need consistent label-input pairing with accessibility semantics
When you need to mark fields as required and show help text
avoid: Avoid for non-form UI layouts — this is specifically for form field wrapping

props.name: children
props.description: The form field component(s) to render on the right side.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class.

props.name: direction
props.description: Layout direction. Defaults to 'row'.

props.name: fieldId
props.description: ID of the associated input element, used to generate a <label> with htmlFor.

props.name: label
props.description: Label text or node.

props.name: labelHelpPopover
props.description: Content placed next to the label, typically a HelpPopover icon.

props.name: required
props.description: Shows a required asterisk next to the label.

examples:
const nameFieldId = 'form-field-name';

<FormRow label="Name" fieldId={nameFieldId} required>
  <TextInput id={nameFieldId} name="name" />
</FormRow>

<FormRow
  label="Description"
  direction="column"
  labelHelpPopover={<HelpPopover content="Describe your item" />}
>
  <TextArea />
  <FormRow.FieldDescription>Max 500 characters</FormRow.FieldDescription>
</FormRow>

name: Gallery
description: A fullscreen modal gallery for images, videos, and documents with built-in keyboard navigation, mobile swipe gestures, fullscreen mode, image zoom/pan, and custom per-item action buttons. Children must be GalleryItem components.
when_to_use: When you need a fullscreen modal to preview images, videos, or documents with navigation
When users need to zoom into images or navigate between files
avoid: Avoid for inline thumbnail grids — Gallery is a modal overlay component
Avoid when only a single static image display is needed without navigation
import_statement: import {Gallery, GalleryItem, getGalleryItemImage, getGalleryItemVideo, getGalleryItemDocument} from '@gravity-ui/components';

props.name: open
props.description: Controls gallery modal visibility.

props.name: onOpenChange
props.type: (open: boolean, event?: Event, reason?: string) => void
props.description: Called when the gallery open state changes.

props.name: children
props.description: GalleryItem elements defining the gallery contents.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the modal.

props.name: container
props.description: DOM container for the modal portal.

props.name: emptyMessage
props.description: Message shown when no items are provided.

props.name: initialItemIndex
props.description: Index of the item to display first. Defaults to 0.

examples:
<Gallery open={open} onOpenChange={(v) => setOpen(v)}>
  {images.map((src, i) => (
    <GalleryItem key={i} {...getGalleryItemImage({src, name: src})} />
  ))}
</Gallery>

<Gallery open={open} onOpenChange={(v) => setOpen(v)}>
  <GalleryItem
    {...getGalleryItemVideo({src: videoUrl, name: 'Demo Video'})}
    actions={[
      getGalleryItemDownloadAction({downloadUrl: videoUrl}),
    ]}
  />
</Gallery>

name: useGallery
description: A React hook that returns the Gallery context, providing openGallery and closeGallery functions along with the current open state. Must be used inside a GalleryProvider.
when_to_use: When you need to imperatively open the gallery from anywhere inside a GalleryProvider tree
When you need to read whether the gallery is currently open
avoid: Do not use outside of a GalleryProvider — the context defaults are no-ops
signature: function useGallery(): { openGallery: (items: GalleryItemProps[], initialFileIndex?: number) => void; closeGallery: () => void; open: boolean }
return_type: { openGallery: (items: GalleryItemProps[], initialFileIndex?: number) => void; closeGallery: () => void; open: boolean }

name: InfiniteScroll
description: Wraps a list of children and triggers an onActivate callback with a loading state when the user scrolls to the bottom. Useful for social feeds and paginated lists that load more data on scroll.
when_to_use: When you have a long list that loads data in pages triggered by scrolling to the end
When you want to replace a 'Load more' button with automatic scroll-triggered loading
avoid: Avoid when the full data set is small enough to render at once
Avoid when users need precise control over when more data loads — use a manual 'Load more' button instead

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, the scroll trigger is inactive. Set to true when all data is loaded.

props.name: onActivate
props.description: Called when the bottom sentinel is intersected. Shows the loader until the returned promise resolves.

props.name: children
props.description: The list content rendered above the sentinel.

props.name: loader
props.description: Custom loading indicator. Defaults to a centered Loader size='l'.

name: ItemSelector
description: A two-panel component for forming a subset from a list. The left panel shows available items with a search filter and 'Select' buttons; the right panel shows selected items in a sortable list with remove buttons.
when_to_use: When users need to choose and reorder a subset of items from a larger list (e.g., visible columns, participants)
When the selection order matters and needs drag-and-drop reordering
avoid: Avoid for small option sets — a MultiSelect or Checkbox group is simpler
Avoid when items cannot be identified by a string ID

props.name: items
props.description: Full list of available items.

props.name: onUpdate
props.description: Called with the updated array of selected item IDs when selection changes.

props.name: value
props.description: Array of currently selected item IDs.

props.name: filterItem
props.description: Custom filter function for search. Defaults to matching by getItemId.

props.name: getItemId
props.description: Returns the unique string ID for an item. Defaults to String(item).

props.name: hideSelectAllButton
props.description: Hides the 'Select all' button.

props.name: hideSelected
props.description: When true (default), already-selected items are hidden from the available list.

props.name: renderItem
props.description: Custom render function for items in the available list.

props.name: renderItemValue
props.description: Custom render function for item labels in both panels.

props.name: renderValueItem
props.description: Custom render function for items in the selected list.

props.name: selectorTitle
props.description: Title shown above the available items list.

examples:
<ItemSelector
  selectorTitle="Columns"
  items={[
    {name: 'id', type: 'Uint32'},
    {name: 'series', type: 'Utf8'},
    {name: 'title', type: 'String'},
  ]}
  value={selected}
  onUpdate={setSelected}
  getItemId={(item) => item.name}
  renderItemValue={(item) => `${item.name} (${item.type})`}
/>

name: Notifications
description: Renders a notification feed panel with loading, empty, and error states, infinite scroll support, and swipeable notification cards. Supports side actions, bottom actions, and mobile swipe gestures per notification.
when_to_use: When you need a notification panel inside a popup or drawer with loading, empty, and error states
When notifications should support infinite scroll and swipe-to-action on mobile
avoid: Avoid for toast/snackbar-style transient alerts — use uikit Toaster instead
Avoid when a simple list without loading states is sufficient

props.name: notifications
props.description: List of notification objects to render.

props.name: actions
props.description: Action controls shown in the header (e.g., 'Mark all as read').

props.name: areAllNotificationsLoaded
props.description: When true, disables the infinite scroll trigger.

props.name: emptyContent
props.description: Content shown in the empty state.

props.name: emptyImage
props.description: Custom image for the empty state.

props.name: emptyTitle
props.description: Title for the empty state. Defaults to 'No notifications'.

props.name: errorContent
props.description: Content shown in the error state.

props.name: errorImage
props.description: Custom image for the error state.

props.name: errorTitle
props.description: Title for the error state. Defaults to 'Error'.

props.name: isLoading
props.description: Shows a loading spinner when true.

props.name: onLoadMoreNotifications
props.description: Called by InfiniteScroll when the user scrolls to the bottom.

props.name: renderCustomHeader
props.description: Custom header renderer receiving the title node.

props.name: swipeThreshold
props.description: Fraction (0–1) of width needed to trigger a swipe action. Defaults to 0.4.

props.name: title
props.description: Panel title. Defaults to 'Notifications'.

examples:
<NotificationsPopupWrapper>
  <Notifications
    title="Notifications"
    notifications={notifications}
    actions={<NotificationAction action={{icon: Plus, text: 'Add', onClick: handleAdd}} />}
    emptyContent="No notifications yet"
  />
</NotificationsPopupWrapper>

name: OnboardingMenu
description: An expandable onboarding progress widget showing a progress bar and a list of onboarding tasks (OnboardingMenu.Item). Supports expand/collapse, optional close button, and complete/collapse action buttons.
when_to_use: When you need to guide new users through onboarding steps with a progress indicator
When integrating with @gravity-ui/onboarding preset system
avoid: Avoid for general navigation menus — this is specifically for onboarding flows
Avoid when a simple checklist without progress is sufficient

props.name: onCompleteClick
props.description: Called when the 'Complete' button is clicked.

props.name: progress
props.description: Progress value (0–100) displayed in the progress bar.

props.name: title
props.description: Title of the onboarding menu.

props.name: expanded
props.description: Controlled expanded state.

props.name: children
props.description: OnboardingMenu.Item components.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class.

props.name: collapseButtonText
props.description: Label for the collapse button.

props.name: completeButtonText
props.description: Label for the complete button.

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon shown next to the title.

props.name: liningClassName
props.description: CSS class for the items container.

props.name: onCloseClick
props.description: Called when the close button (shown on hover) is clicked.

props.name: onCollapseClick
props.description: Called when the collapse button is clicked.

props.name: onExpand
props.description: Called when the expanded state changes.

props.name: withCloseButton
props.description: Shows a close button on hover when collapsed.

examples:
<OnboardingMenu
  title="Getting Started"
  progress={50}
  expanded={expanded}
  onExpand={setExpanded}
  onCompleteClick={handleComplete}
>
  <OnboardingMenu.Item title="Step 1" status="completed">
    <OnboardingMenu.ItemText text="First step is done!" />
  </OnboardingMenu.Item>
  <OnboardingMenu.Item title="Step 2" status="pending" hasDivider={false}>
    <OnboardingMenu.ItemText text="Complete your profile" />
  </OnboardingMenu.Item>
</OnboardingMenu>

name: PromoSheet
description: A mobile bottom sheet for promoting a new feature or app. Renders a title, message text, optional image, and action/close buttons using the uikit Sheet component.
when_to_use: When you need to inform mobile users about a new feature in a non-blocking bottom sheet
When promoting an app download or new service feature on mobile
avoid: Avoid on desktop — this uses a Sheet which is primarily a mobile pattern
Avoid for critical blocking confirmations — use ConfirmDialog instead

props.name: actionText
props.description: Label for the action button.

props.name: closeText
props.description: Accessible label for the close button (used as aria-label).

props.name: message
props.description: Body text of the promo sheet.

props.name: title
props.description: Title of the promo sheet.

props.name: actionHref
props.description: URL for the action button.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class.

props.name: contentClassName
props.description: CSS class for the sheet content area.

props.name: imageAlt
props.description: Alt text for the image.

props.name: imageClassName
props.description: CSS class for the image element.

props.name: imageContainerClassName
props.description: CSS class for the image container.

props.name: imageSrc
props.description: URL of the promotional image.

props.name: onActionClick
props.description: Called when the action button is clicked.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Called when the sheet closes.

examples:
<PromoSheet
  title="Try our mobile app!"
  message="Get the best experience on your phone."
  actionText="Download now"
  closeText="Close"
  actionHref="https://example.com/app"
  imageSrc="/promo.png"
/>

name: Reactions
description: Renders a row of emoji reaction buttons with counters and an 'Add reaction' button that opens a palette popup. Supports read-only mode, custom tooltips showing who reacted, and fully custom render functions for reactions and layout.
when_to_use: When you need GitHub-style emoji reactions on content like comments, messages, or posts
When you want users to express quick feedback with predefined emoji options
avoid: Avoid when only a single like/dislike is needed — use a toggle Button instead
Avoid for ratings — use a dedicated rating component

props.name: reactions
props.description: All available reactions for the palette (PaletteOption array with content and value).

props.name: reactionsState
props.description: Current reaction state array with value, counter, and selected flags.

props.name: addButtonPlacement
props.description: Position of the 'Add reaction' button. Defaults to 'end'.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the reactions container.

props.name: onToggle
props.description: Called when the user clicks a reaction in the list or palette.

props.name: paletteProps
props.description: Props forwarded to the Palette component.

props.name: popupClassName
props.description: CSS class for the palette popup.

props.name: popupPlacement
props.description: Placement of the palette popup.

props.name: qa
props.description: Data-qa attribute for testing.

props.name: readOnly
props.description: When true, hides the add button and disables toggling.

props.name: renderAddReaction
props.description: Custom render function for the add reaction button.

props.name: renderReaction
props.description: Custom render function for individual reaction buttons.

props.name: renderReactionsContent
props.description: Full custom layout renderer for the reactions area.

props.name: renderTooltip
props.description: Renders a tooltip (e.g., list of users) when hovering a reaction.

props.name: size
props.description: Button size. Defaults to 'm'.

props.name: style
props.description: Inline styles for the container.

examples:
<Reactions
  reactions={[
    {content: '👍', value: 'thumbs-up'},
    {content: '😎', value: 'cool'},
  ]}
  reactionsState={[
    {value: 'cool', counter: 2, selected: true},
  ]}
  onToggle={handleToggle}
/>

(
{reactedUsers[state.value]?.map(u => u.name).join(', ')}
)} readOnly={!isLoggedIn} />

name: SharePopover
description: A sharing button that opens a popover with social network share links and/or a copy-link button. Supports Web Share API, configurable share options (Telegram, Facebook, Twitter, VK, LinkedIn), hover or click trigger, and custom copy rendering.
when_to_use: When you need a share button with social network options and/or a copy-URL action
When you want native OS share sheet via Web Share API on supported devices
avoid: Avoid when only a single share target is needed — use a direct link button instead
Avoid without a URL to share

props.name: url
props.description: The URL to share.

props.name: autoclosable
props.description: Closes popover when cursor leaves. Defaults to true.

props.name: buttonAriaLabel
props.description: Accessible aria-label for the share button.

props.name: buttonTitle
props.description: Text label shown next to the share icon.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the root element.

props.name: closeDelay
props.description: Delay in ms before closing on hover-out. Defaults to 300.

props.name: copyIcon
props.description: Custom icon for the copy link button.

props.name: copyTitle
props.description: Custom label for the copy link button.

props.name: customIcon
props.description: Custom icon for the share trigger button.

props.name: direction
props.description: Direction of share options layout.

props.name: iconClass
props.description: CSS class for the icon.

props.name: iconSize
props.description: Size of the share icon in px. Defaults to 16.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Custom click handler for the trigger button.

props.name: openByHover
props.description: Opens popover on hover. Defaults to true.

props.name: placement
props.description: Popover placement. Defaults to ['bottom-end'].

props.name: renderCopy
props.description: Custom render function for the copy link item.

props.name: shareOptions
props.description: Social network share options to display.

props.name: switcherClassName
props.description: CSS class for the trigger button.

props.name: text
props.description: Share text (used in Web Share API and some social links).

props.name: title
props.description: Share title (used in social link metadata).

props.name: tooltipClassName
props.description: CSS class for the popover tooltip.

props.name: useWebShareApi
props.description: Uses native Web Share API when supported. Defaults to false.

props.name: withCopyLink
props.description: Shows a copy link button. Defaults to true.

examples: <SharePopover url={url} title={title} text={text} />

<SharePopover
  url={url}
  title={title}
  text={text}
  withCopyLink={false}
  shareOptions={[
    ShareOptions.Telegram,
    ShareOptions.Twitter,
    ShareOptions.VK,
  ]}
/>

<SharePopover
  url={url}
  title={title}
  text={text}
  useWebShareApi={true}
/>

name: StoreBadge
description: Renders an App Store or Google Play badge as a link (or clickable block when no href is provided), localized in English or Russian.
when_to_use: When you need standardized App Store or Google Play download badges on a landing page or promotional section
When linking users to your mobile app from a web page
avoid: Avoid for non-mobile-app contexts — this is specifically for app store links

props.name: platform
props.description: Target app store platform.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class.

props.name: href
props.description: URL of the app store listing. Without this, renders as a non-link button.

props.name: lang
props.description: Badge language. Defaults to 'en'.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Click handler.

examples: <StoreBadge platform="android" />
<StoreBadge platform="ios" href="https://apps.apple.com/app/id123" />
<StoreBadge platform="android" lang="ru" href="https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.example" />

name: Stories
description: Displays a fullscreen story carousel modal with text content, media (image or video), navigation arrows, keyboard support, and optional cross-tab sync via localStorage. Each StoriesItem can have custom action buttons and styling.
when_to_use: When you need to showcase product features or onboarding steps in a Stories-style fullscreen modal
When you want to display a sequence of media with text overlays
avoid: Avoid for displaying grouped stories from multiple sources — use StoriesGroup instead
Avoid for inline image carousels — Stories is a fullscreen modal

props.name: items
props.description: Array of story items to display.

props.name: open
props.description: Controls modal visibility.

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class for the modal content.

props.name: disableOutsideClick
props.description: Prevents closing on outside click. Defaults to true.

props.name: initialStoryIndex
props.description: Index of the first story to show. Defaults to 0.

props.name: onClose
props.type: (event: MouseEvent | KeyboardEvent | React.MouseEvent, reason: ModalCloseReason | 'closeButtonClick') => void
props.description: Called when the modal closes.

props.name: onNextClick
props.description: Called when the user navigates to the next story.

props.name: onPreviousClick
props.description: Called when the user navigates to the previous story.

props.name: resetStoryIndexOnClose
props.description: Resets to initialStoryIndex when the modal closes.

props.name: syncInTabsKey
props.description: When set, syncs close action across browser tabs via localStorage.

examples:
setOpen(false)} items={[ { title: 'New Feature', content:

Here is what is new.

, media: {type: 'image', url: '/feature.png'}, }, { title: 'Another Update', media: {type: 'video', url: '/update.mp4'}, }, ]} />

name: StoriesGroup
description: Extends Stories with a sidebar of group thumbnails, allowing users to navigate between multiple story groups each containing multiple items. Renders a two-panel fullscreen modal.
when_to_use: When you have multiple named groups of stories that users should be able to jump between via thumbnails
When your story content is organized into categories or topics
avoid: Avoid when there is only one story group — use Stories for simplicity
Avoid when group thumbnails would add unnecessary visual complexity

props.name: groups
props.description: Array of story groups, each containing items and optional thumbnail media.

props.name: open
props.description: Controls modal visibility.

props.name: disableOutsideClick
props.description: Prevents closing on outside click. Defaults to true.

props.name: initialStoryIndex
props.description: Initial [group, item] index tuple. Defaults to [0, 0].

props.name: maxSliderItemsCount
props.description: Maximum number of group thumbnails shown in the slider. Defaults to 12.

props.name: onClose
props.type: (event: MouseEvent | KeyboardEvent | React.MouseEvent, reason: ModalCloseReason | 'closeButtonClick') => void
props.description: Called when the modal closes.

props.name: onItemSelect
props.type: (itemIndexes: [groupIndex: number, itemIndex: number], selectedFromThumbnail: boolean) => void
props.description: Called when a story item is selected, including whether it was via thumbnail.

examples:
<StoriesGroup
  open={open}
  onClose={() => setOpen(false)}
  groups={[
    {
      items: [
        {
          title: 'Feature A',
          media: {type: 'image', url: '/feature-a.png'},
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      items: [
        {
          title: 'Feature B',
          media: {type: 'video', url: '/feature-b.mp4'},
        },
      ],
    },
  ]}
/>
